titleStmt.title: Letter from John Elliott and Edward Clark to Henry T. Gallagher, 23 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Elliott

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Elliot & Clark Solicitors to Henry T. Gallagher informing him that they will be acting as defence in the case of King vs Bonner & Ors. and providing the list of witnesses for the trial.
John Elliott (1859-1936) was a solcitor in the firm of Messrs Elliot ad Clark in Strabane, Co. Tyrone. Elliott's only son Cecil Moore Elliott was a private in Private "D" Coy. 7th Battalion of The Royal Dublin Fusiliers. He was killed in action in August 1915 aged twenty-two.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
ELLIOTT & CLARK
SOLICTORS.
JOHN ELLIOTT,
Coroner for North Tyrone
EDWARD CLARKE
Deputy Coroner.
Telegrams- "ELLIOTT, CORONER."
Telephone No 24. STRABANE,
CO.TYRONE. 23rd June, 1916. King V Bonner & Ors. Dear Sir, We are acting for the defence in above case at next
Lifford Assizes and wish to summon as witnesses for the defence Edward
Brennan of Aughmeen, Dungloe and Patrick Sweeney of Cranashallow,
Dungloe who in our opinion are material witnesses. Our clients are poor
people and as they will incure very heavy expense in the defence and
in travelling from Dungloe to Lifford, we would ask you to have the
two witnesses summoned by the Crown. Yours faithfully, Elliott & Clarke H.T. Gallagher Esq.,
Crown Solictor,
Strabane.
